title: NARC Field Day
text:
This is a website that describes planning for the ARRL Field Day operation conducted by the Nittany Amateur Radio Club (NARC). Rick Gilmore W3TM created and maintains the site.
Please see separate pages that describe what Field Day is about, the people who are involved, what they do when to make Field Day happen, and some of the material items needed to pull-off an event like this.



Antennas at sunset.








Note



The event runs for 24 hours beginning at 2:00 PM EDT (1800 UTC) on Saturday to 2:00 PM EDT (1800 UTC) on Sunday.

title: About
text:
Field Day is an event organized by the American Radio Relay League (ARRL), the membership organization for amateur radio operators (hams) in the U.S.
Field Day is an open house to show-off amateur radio to the public, an exercise in portable or field radio operations, and a contest to see which stations can earn the most points.






ImportantImportant details



Field Day 2026 runs from 2:00 pm (EDT) on Saturday, June 27 to 2:00 pm on Sunday, June 28, 2026.
Field Day is open to the public, so please visit us. See the map below.


NARC will operate under the club callsign W3YA with one or two high frequency (HF) transmitters capable of long-distance communication.
We may have a bonus VHF/UHF station depending on member interest.
We will operate from the Pleasant Gap Fire Company Carnival Grounds at 375 Robinson Lane in Pleasant Gap, PA.



W3YA @ Field Day

title: Results
text:
About
This page provides some data about the club’s activities and results on Field Day.

title: Results
Results
text:
QSOs/transmitter


Code
w3ya |>
  dplyr::mutate(qs_per_xmtr = tot_qsos/n_xmtrs) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = year, y = qs_per_xmtr) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2012, 2026, by = 1))










Participants


Code
w3ya |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(n_particip)) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = year, y = n_particip) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2012, 2026, by = 1))


`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'


Code
  ylim(0,60)


<ScaleContinuousPosition>
 Range:  
 Limits:    0 --   60








Figure 3.7: Nittany ARC (W3YA) Field Day participants by year

title: Results
text:
QSOs/participant


Code
w3ya |>
  dplyr::mutate(qs_per_person = tot_qsos/n_particip) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = year, y = qs_per_person) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2012, 2026, by = 1))

title: Recent Years
text:
About
This page summarizes data about club member participation in 2024-25.

title: Recent Years
text:
Setup

library(readr)
library(ggplot2)

title: Recent Years
text:
Gather data

fd_24 <- readr::read_csv(
  "csv/narc-fd-2024-participation.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  col_types = cols(
    callsign = "c",
    attended_sat = "l",
    attended_sun = "l",
    operated_fd = "l",
    helped_out = "l"
  )
)

Warning: One or more parsing issues, call `problems()` on your data frame for details,
e.g.:
  dat <- vroom(...)
  problems(dat)

fd_24 <- fd_24 |>
  dplyr::mutate(attended = attended_sat | attended_sun)


fd_25 <- readr::read_csv(
  "csv/narc-field-day-2025-participation.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  col_types = cols(
    callsign = "c",
    member = "l",
    operated_fd = "l",
    setup_cleanup = "l",
    late_overnight = "l",
    pr = "l",
    food_drink = "l",
    education = "l",
    attended_sat = "l",
    attended_sun = "l",
    operated_fd = "l"
  )
)

fd_25 <- fd_25 |>
  dplyr::mutate(helped_out = setup_cleanup | pr | food_drink | education )


fd_24 <- fd_24 |>
  dplyr::select(callsign, attended_sat, attended_sun, operated_fd, helped_out) |>
  dplyr::mutate(year = 2024,
                callsign = toupper(callsign))

fd_25 <- fd_25 |>
  dplyr::select(callsign, attended_sat, attended_sun, operated_fd, helped_out) |>
  dplyr::mutate(year = 2025,
                callsign = toupper(callsign))

fd_24_25 <- rbind(fd_24, fd_25) |>
  dplyr::mutate(attended = attended_sat | attended_sun)

fd_24_25_ns <- fd_24_25 |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarise(n_attended = sum(attended),
                   n_operated = sum(operated_fd),
                   n_helped = sum(helped_out, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Recent Years
Member Survey
text:
About
This page documents some of the steps in our planning process.
W3TM drafted and distributed on May 18, 2026 a member survey using a Google Form. Here are some of the preliminary results.

title: Member Survey
text:
Note



To reveal the code that generates the following outputs, select the ‘Show All Code’ item from the ‘</> Code’ dropdown menu at upper right.








Important



Thank you to <hamqth.com> for providing a free and open callsign lookup API.

title: Member Survey
text:
Set-up
We source some packages to make calls to their functions more convenient.


Code
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
knitr::opts_chunk$set(tidy = "styler")




Code
generate_callsign_url <- function(callsign = "W3TM") {
  assertthat::is.string(callsign)
  paste0("<a href='https://www.hamqth.com/s.php?callsign=", callsign, "'>", callsign, "</a>")
}

title: Member Survey
text:
Gathering
The Google Form application saves the survey responses as a Google Sheet:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScAu-pfcYEfVoUwy_xHOlA3KDA3ZfFuiYYJRorVdLNXfahjSQ/viewform?usp=header


Code
if (!dir.exists("csv")) {
  message("Creating missing `csv/`.")
  dir.create("csv")
}

update_figs <- TRUE

full_survey_data_fn <- file.path("csv", params$survey_data_fn)

if (params$update_data) {
  options(gargle_oauth_email = Sys.getenv("GMAIL_ROG"))
  googledrive::drive_auth()

  new_fn <- file.path("csv", paste0(params$survey_data_fn))
  fd26 <- googlesheets4::gs4_find() |> dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name, "Field Day 2026"))

  fd_2026 <- googlesheets4::read_sheet(ss = fd26$id, sheet = "Form Responses 1")
  x <- assertthat::assert_that(is_tibble(fd_2026))
  message("Data updated.")
  readr::write_csv(fd_2026, file = new_fn)
} else {
  if (!file.exists(full_survey_data_fn)) {
    warning("File not found: '", full_survey_data_fn, "'.")
    update_figs <- FALSE
  } else {
    message("Using previously stored data.")
  }
}


✔ Reading from "Field Day 2026 volunteer sign-up (Responses)".


✔ Range ''Form Responses 1''.


Data updated.


Now, we load the downloaded CSV.


Code
if (update_figs) {
  survey_resps <-
    readr::read_csv(full_survey_data_fn, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

names(survey_resps)


 [1] "Timestamp"                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 [2] "Your Name"                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 [3] "Callsign"                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
 [4] "Phone"                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
 [5] "Email"                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
 [6] "Do you plan to attend NARC's Field Day 2025?"                                                                                                                                                                                       
 [7] "Do you plan to operate on Field Day?"                                                                                                                                                                                               
 [8] "Are you a night owl and want to operate late into the night or camp out to snag some rare DX?"                                                                                                                                      
 [9] "If you plan to operate, which of the following mode(s) would interest you? \n\nNote: We will have knowledgeable helpers for all modes we use on Field Day, so you are encouraged to try a new mode and will have help if you do so."
[10] "I'm interested in helping with the following activities to promote Field Day and amateur radio to the public..."                                                                                                                    
[11] "Comments or suggestions"                                                                                                                                                                                                            








ImportantResponse rate



We had n=18 responses to the survey. Two other members reached out directly via email.

title: Member Survey
text:
Cleaning
Then we clean the data by renaming the variables.


Code
survey_resps <- survey_resps |>
  dplyr::rename(
    timestamp = "Timestamp",
    name = "Your Name",
    callsign = "Callsign",
    phone = "Phone",
    email = "Email",
    attend = "Do you plan to attend NARC's Field Day 2025?",
    operating = "Do you plan to operate on Field Day?",
    overnight = "Are you a night owl and want to operate late into the night or camp out to snag some rare DX?",
    help_with = "I'm interested in helping with the following activities to promote Field Day and amateur radio to the public...",
    comments = "Comments or suggestions"
  )

names(survey_resps)[9] <- "modes"


Then we parse variables with multiple values, and conforming some values so that they are easier to visualize.


Code
survey_resps <- survey_resps |>
  # Change timestamp to proper date and time
  dplyr::mutate(timestamp = lubridate::ymd_hms(survey_resps$timestamp, tz = "America/New_York")) |>
  # Change values for 'attend' to make them more easily readable
  dplyr::mutate(when = attend) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    when = case_when(
      when == "Yes, on Saturday June 27" ~ "sat",
      when == "Yes, on Sunday June 28" ~ "sun",
      when == "Yes, on Saturday June 27, Yes, on Sunday June 28" ~ "sat_sun",
      when == "Not sure yet" ~ "not_sure",
      when == "Sorry, not this year" ~ "not_coming"
    )
  ) |>
  # Change values for 'operate' to make them lower case
  dplyr::mutate(operate = tolower(operating)) |>
  # Change values for 'overnight'
  dplyr::mutate(overnight = dplyr::recode(
    overnight,
    "Yes, absolutely" = TRUE,
    "Heck no" = FALSE
  )) |>
  # Create new variable for each mode
  dplyr::mutate(
    phone = stringr::str_detect(modes, "Phone"),
    cw = stringr::str_detect(modes, "CW"),
    ft8 = stringr::str_detect(modes, "FT8/FT4"),
    varac = stringr::str_detect(modes, "VarAC"),
    fldigi = stringr::str_detect(modes, "fldigi"),
    js8call = stringr::str_detect(modes, "JS8Call"),
    sstv = stringr::str_detect(modes, "SSTV")
  ) |>
  # Create new variables for each volunteer role
  dplyr::mutate(
    pr = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "PR"),
    info_table = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "table"),
    satellite = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "satellite"),
    mode_expert = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "expert"),
    ed_activity = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Educational"),
    invite_elected = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "elected"),
    youth = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "youth"),
    food = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "food"),
    clean_up = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "clean-up"),
    test_equipment = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "equipment")
  )

survey_resps$overnight[is.na(survey_resps$overnight)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$pr[is.na(survey_resps$pr)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$info_table[is.na(survey_resps$info_table)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$satellite[is.na(survey_resps$satellite)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$mode_expert[is.na(survey_resps$mode_expert)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$ed_activity[is.na(survey_resps$ed_activity)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$invite_elected[is.na(survey_resps$invite_elected)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$youth[is.na(survey_resps$youth)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$food[is.na(survey_resps$food)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$clean_up[is.na(survey_resps$clean_up)] <- FALSE
survey_resps$test_equipment[is.na(survey_resps$test_equipment)] <- FALSE


It will be easier if we make separate variables for attendance on Sat and Sun, so we do that now.


Code
survey_resps <- survey_resps |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    attend_sat = stringr::str_detect(when, "sat"),
    attend_sun = stringr::str_detect(when, "sun")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(attend_any = (attend_sat | attend_sun))


We make all the callsigns uppercase for consistency.


Code
survey_resps <- survey_resps |>
  dplyr::mutate(callsign = toupper(callsign))


Finally, we export the cleaned file.


Code
readr::write_csv(survey_resps, file.path("csv", paste0("clean_", params$survey_data_fn)))

title: Member Survey
text:
Visualizing
As of 2026-07-14 08:43:36.997134, we’ve had 18 responses.

Time series


Code
survey_resps |>
  dplyr::select(timestamp, attend_any) |>
  dplyr::mutate(order = seq_along(timestamp)) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = timestamp, y = order, color = attend_any)) +
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x = timestamp, y = order)) +
  ylab("n") +
  xlab("Response date")








Figure 5.1: Time series of responses to the Field Day survey with an indication about whether the respondents intend to attend either of the two operating days indicated by color code: Red/FALSE indicating no and Green/TRUE indicating yes.







Attending and operating
Here are the number of respondents who plan to attend and their plans to operate.


Code
xtabs(formula = ~ when + operate, data = survey_resps)


            operate
when         maybe no yes
  not_coming     0  0   1
  not_sure       1  1   2
  sat            2  0   1
  sat_sun        1  1   6
  sun            0  0   1




Code
survey_resps <- survey_resps |>
  dplyr::mutate(callsign_url = generate_callsign_url(callsign))

survey_resps |>
  dplyr::mutate(callsign_url = generate_callsign_url(callsign)) |>
  dplyr::select(callsign_url, attend_sat, overnight, attend_sun) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(attend_sat), !is.na(overnight), !is.na(attend_sun)) |>
  kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE) |>
  kableExtra::kable_classic() |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(2, color = ifelse(survey_resps$attend_sat == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(4, color = ifelse(survey_resps$attend_sun == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(3, color = ifelse(survey_resps$overnight == TRUE, "green", "red"))

We can expect n=11 to attend Saturday, n=6 to operate into the night, and n=9 to attend Sunday.


Modes
Here are respondents’ interests by mode:


Code
attendees <- survey_resps |>
  dplyr::filter(attend_any == TRUE)

modes <- attendees |>
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(callsign),
    !is.na(phone),
    !is.na(cw),
    !is.na(ft8),
    !is.na(varac),
    !is.na(fldigi),
    !is.na(js8call),
    !is.na(sstv)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(callsign_url, phone, cw, ft8, varac, fldigi, js8call, sstv)

modes |>
  kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE) |>

Volunteering


Code
survey_resps <- survey_resps |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    help_with_pr = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "PR/publicity"),
    help_with_public_info = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Public information table"),
    help_with_satellite_qso = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Satellite QSO"),
    help_with_mode_elmer = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "expert"),
    help_with_ed_activity = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Educational activity"),
    help_with_elected_officials = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Inviting elected officials or agency reps to visit"),
    help_with_youth = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Coordinating youth participation or visits by Scout troops or other individuals"),
    help_with_food_drink = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Planning and/or buying/preparing food and drink"),
    help_with_setup_cleanup = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Set-up or clean-up"),
    help_with_testing_gear = stringr::str_detect(help_with, "Testing equipment")
  )

attendees <- survey_resps

volunteers <- attendees |>
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(help_with_pr),
    !is.na(help_with_public_info),
    !is.na(help_with_satellite_qso),
    !is.na(help_with_mode_elmer),
    !is.na(help_with_ed_activity),
    !is.na(help_with_elected_officials),
    !is.na(help_with_youth),
    !is.na(help_with_setup_cleanup),
    !is.na(help_with_testing_gear)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    callsign_url, help_with_pr, help_with_public_info, help_with_satellite_qso,
    help_with_mode_elmer, help_with_ed_activity, help_with_elected_officials,
    help_with_youth, help_with_setup_cleanup,
    help_with_testing_gear
  )

names(volunteers) <- stringr::str_remove(names(volunteers), "help_with_")

# Hand-modify data frame

volunteers |>
  kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE) |>
  kableExtra::kable_classic() |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(column = 2, color = ifelse(volunteers$pr == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(column = 3, color = ifelse(volunteers$public_info == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(column = 4, color = ifelse(volunteers$satellite_qso == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(column = 5, color = ifelse(volunteers$mode_elmer == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>
  kableExtra::column_spec(column = 6, color = ifelse(volunteers$ed_activity == TRUE, "green", "red")) |>

  kableExtra::column_spec(column = 10, color = ifelse(volunteers$testing_gear == TRUE, "green", "red"))

title: Calendar
text:
About
This page describes some of the specific actions that need to be taken leading up to and on Field Day.

title: Calendar
text:
May 2026

Add info to ARRL Field Day website. (W3TM)
Create Field Day budget (W3TM)

Food & drink budget.
Porta-Potty estimate. (K3CWP)
Equipment estimate.
PR and supply estimate.

Secure NARC BoD budget approval. (W3TM & K3YV).

title: Calendar
text:
June 2026

4 weeks out

Add event to web-based activity calendars.

WSPU activity calendar. (W3TM)
State College dot com calendar. (H3LP)

Send invitations to other groups

Pack 67 Cub Scouts. (W3TM)
Troop XXX Scouts (K3IWI per 2026-06-19 text)
Troop 375 (KD3CCP per email)
Central PA Observers. (W3TM; 2026-06-23)
Announce on ARES net (W3EDP)
new-faculty-l (W3TM; 2026-06-23)

Draft and send invitations to elected officials. (W3TM; 2026-06-23)
Draft and send invitation to served agency official. (W3TM)
Schedule Porta-Potty delivery (K3CWP).

Robinson booked 2026-06-08 (K3CWP).

Distribute member survey (W3TM).
Configure & test computers (W3TM)

title: Stations
text:
About
This page describes some details about our Field Day stations.

title: Stations
text:
Class
Based on the results of the survey, we plan to operate two (2) transmitters.






NoteGet on the Air (GOTA) station



On 2026-05-26 the Board discussed whether to mount a GOTA station this year. We decided to use one of our transmitters specifically for helping new operators or inactive operators get on the air.


We will operate at 100 W and use W3YA as our callsign.

title: Stations
text:
Radios
Our plan is to configure separate radios for phone, digital modes, and CW. Radios configured for CW and digital modes have some special characteristics that make it more difficult to switch in the field.

Station-1

ICOM IC-7610 transceiver
Mini PC with external display, keyboard, mouse
Halibut Electronics audio interface?
ICOM SM-50 mic
Headset(s)
Footswitch
Power supply



Station-2

Yaesu FT-710 (loaned by K3JHG; W3TM has been testing)
NARC2 Mini PC with external display, keyboard, mouse
Mic

Yaesu hand mic

Headset(s)
Footswitch
Power supply

title: Stations
text:
Filters
The club owns bandpass filters for 75m/80m, 40m, 20m, 15m, and 10m. All radios will use bandpass filters on Field Day.

title: Power estimation
text:
About
This page documents estimations about power usage for Field Day.

title: Power estimation
text:
Setup

library(googlesheets4)


gs4_auth(email = params$google_email)

title: Power estimation
text:
Import
W3TM generated a Google Sheet with data for these estimates:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10F2JfQ3A6oFc3VFz_GTDg_t_ZwVtrC2EGjVt0kopYQU/edit?gid=0#gid=0

if (params$update_data) {
  gs4_auth(email = params$google_email)
  power <- read_sheet(params$sheet_url)
  
  readr::write_csv(power, file = "csv/power-estimation.csv")
} else {
  message("Data not updated. Using stored copy")
}

Data not updated. Using stored copy



power <- readr::read_csv(file = "csv/power-estimation.csv", show_col_types = TRUE)

Rows: 32 Columns: 11
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr  (1): mode
dbl (10): xmit_watts, xmit_amps, duty_cycle, AperHr, battery_Ah, op_hrs_batt...

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

title: Power estimation
text:
Summarize
The modes summarized here include: FT8, ph.
Power levels estimated for the modes are as follows:

xtabs(~ mode + xmit_watts, data = power)

     xmit_watts
mode  40 100
  FT8 16   0
  ph   0  16


We’re more likely to run 40W on FT8.
Duty cycles for these modes are as follows:

xtabs(~ mode + duty_cycle, data = power)

     duty_cycle
mode  0.25 0.3 0.5
  FT8    8   0   8
  ph     8   8   0


The 0.5 duty cycle for FT8 and 0.3 duty cycle for phone assume pretty aggressive running on a frequency. More realistic duty cycles are 0.25 for phone FT8.
Operating hours are estimated as follows:

xtabs(~ op_hrs_ham, data = power)

op_hrs_ham
12 15 18 24 
 8  8  8  8

title: Power estimation
section: Scenario: Modest duty cycles + 12 hour operating
text:
Scenario: Modest duty cycles + 12 hour operating

power |>
  dplyr::filter(op_hrs_ham == 12,
                duty_cycle == .25) |>
  dplyr::select(mode, battery_Ah, op_hrs_battery, batt_24hrs)

# A tibble: 4 × 4
  mode  battery_Ah op_hrs_battery batt_24hrs
  <chr>      <dbl>          <dbl>      <dbl>
1 FT8           30          13.0        0.92
2 FT8           40          17.4        0.69
3 ph            30           5.22       2.3 
4 ph            40           6.96       1.72


This implies that we would need 1 30 Ah battery to run FT8 for the entire 24 hr period, and 1.7 (round to 2) 40 Ah batteries to run phone the entire 24 hour period.

title: Power estimation
section: Scenario: Modest duty cycles + 18 hour operating
text:
Scenario: Modest duty cycles + 18 hour operating

power |>
  dplyr::filter(op_hrs_ham == 18,
                duty_cycle == .25) |>
  dplyr::select(mode, battery_Ah, op_hrs_battery, batt_24hrs)

# A tibble: 4 × 4
  mode  battery_Ah op_hrs_battery batt_24hrs
  <chr>      <dbl>          <dbl>      <dbl>
1 FT8           30          13.0        1.38
2 FT8           40          17.4        1.03
3 ph            30           5.22       3.45
4 ph            40           6.96       2.59


We would need 2 40 Ah batteries for FT8 and 3 40 Ah batteries to run phone.

title: Power estimation
section: Scenario: Aggressive duty cycles + 24 hour operating
text:
Scenario: Aggressive duty cycles + 24 hour operating

power |>
  dplyr::filter(op_hrs_ham == 24,
                duty_cycle > .25) |>
  dplyr::select(mode, duty_cycle, battery_Ah, op_hrs_battery, batt_24hrs)

# A tibble: 4 × 5
  mode  duty_cycle battery_Ah op_hrs_battery batt_24hrs
  <chr>      <dbl>      <dbl>          <dbl>      <dbl>
1 FT8          0.5         30           6.52       3.68
2 FT8          0.5         40           8.70       2.76
3 ph           0.3         30           4.35       5.52
4 ph           0.3         40           5.80       4.14


Under an aggressive operating and duty cycle schedule, we’d need 3 40 Ah batteries to run FT8 and 5 40 Ah batteries to operate.






Important



These scenarios do not estimate any impact of solar recharging.
However, since recharging really only helps beginning Sunday morning, the impact is only on the daylight hours on Sunday.

title: Power estimation
text:
Batteries available
The club does not own any batteries.
W3TM has the following:

1x 30 Ah
2x 40 Ah

So, W3TM could cover an FT8 and a phone station for Field Day under the modest operating scenario.
For more active operating scenarios, we would need to rely on recharging batteries Sunday morning, more batteries, or larger batteries than W3TM has available.
